_note: Per-lesson role-play scenario. Grounded in Pre-A1/taipei/market dialogue + vocab. Learner plays Anna; computer plays Tom. Each NPC node offers 2-3 branches so the same lesson language can be reached different ways. Consumed by RP.Engine + RP.classifiers.local (see roleplay/).
title: Taipei · The Night Market

npc.text: Wow! The night market is big.

routes.meaning: Anna says she can smell the food.
examples: I can smell the food. Yummy!
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “I can smell the food. Yummy!”

routes.meaning: Anna agrees the market is big.
routes.hint: 💬 You can say “Yes, it is big!” or “I can smell the food. Yummy!”

partial.message: Tell Tom what you smell: “I can smell the food. Yummy!”

off_topic.message: You are at the night market! Say “I can smell the food. Yummy!”

npc.text: So big! I can smell the food too. I am hungry.

routes.meaning: Anna says she can smell the food too.
examples: I can smell the food. Yummy!
Me too, I smell the food.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “I can smell the food. Yummy!”

partial.message: Say what you smell: “I can smell the food. Yummy!”

off_topic.message: The food smells good! Say “I can smell the food. Yummy!”

npc.text: I want some noodles. I am hungry.

routes.meaning: Anna says she wants a big drink because she is thirsty.
examples: I want a big drink. I am thirsty.
I am thirsty. I want a drink.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “I want a big drink. I am thirsty.”
💬 You can say “I want noodles too.” or “I want a big drink. I am thirsty.”

partial.message: Tell Tom what you want: “I want a big drink. I am thirsty.”

off_topic.message: Tom wants noodles. What do you want? Say “I want a big drink. I am thirsty.”

npc.text: Look! We can play a game here.

routes.meaning: Anna says she can win a small toy.
examples: Yes! I can win a small toy.
I want to win a toy.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Yes! I can win a small toy.”

routes.meaning: Anna wants to play the game.
examples: I want to play the game.
routes.hint: 💬 You can say “Yes, let's play!” or “Yes! I can win a small toy.”

partial.message: Tom found a game. Say “Yes! I can win a small toy.”

off_topic.message: You can play a game here! Try “Yes! I can win a small toy.”

npc.text: The food is hot and good.

routes.meaning: Anna says she is happy and this is fun.
examples: I am happy. This is fun.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “I am happy. This is fun.”

partial.message: Tell Tom how you feel: “I am happy. This is fun.”

off_topic.message: The food is hot and good! Say “I am happy. This is fun.”

npc.text: Now I am full. Are you full?

routes.meaning: Anna is full and wants to go to the park next.
examples: Yes! Now let us go to the park.
Yes, I am full. Let's go to the park.
Yes! Let us go to the park.
I am full. To the park!
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Yes! Now let us go to the park.”
💬 You can say “No, not yet.” or “Yes! Now let us go to the park.”

partial.message: Are you full? Say “Yes! Now let us go to the park.”

off_topic.message: Tom asked if you are full. Try “Yes! Now let us go to the park.”

npc.text: Okay! Eat some more. Ready to go now?

learning_objective: Say you are ready to go

routes.meaning: Anna is ready and wants to go to the park.
examples: Yes! Now let us go to the park.
Okay, I am full. Let's go!
Yes, let us go to the park.
I am ready. To the park!
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Yes! Now let us go to the park.”

partial.message: Ready now? Say “Yes! Now let us go to the park.”

off_topic.message: Time to go. Say “Yes! Now let us go to the park.”

npc.text: Great! The night market was fun. Let us go to the park!
